input.background: Chapter 17 considered results from the estrogen trial of the Women's Health Initiative study. The incidence of the combined morbidity index in the treatment group was 751 per 8506 (8.83%). The incidence in the control group was 623 of 8102 (7.69%). The test of H0: p1=p2 yielded z_stat=2.66, P=0.0078 (two sided). We propose to test the same information with a chi-square statistic.
input.question: Test statistic. Table 18.12 lists observed and expected frequencies for the problem. X_stat^2=sum[(Oi-Ei)^2/Ei] = (751-703.712)^2/703.712 + (7755-7802.288)^2/7802.288 + (623-670.288)^2/670.288 + (7479-7431.712)^2/7431.712 = 3.178 + 0.287 + 3.336 + 0.301 = 7.101. This chi-square statistic has df=(R-1)*(C-1)=(2-1)*(2-1)=1. Notice that sqrt(X_stat^2)=sqrt(7.101)=2.66=z_stat.

treatment_group_cases.description: Number of cases in the treatment group.

treatment_group_noncases.description: Number of noncases in the treatment group.

control_group_cases.description: Number of cases in the control group.

control_group_noncases.description: Number of noncases in the control group.

expected_treatment_group_cases.description: Expected number of cases in the treatment group under the null hypothesis.

expected_treatment_group_noncases.description: Expected number of noncases in the treatment group under the null hypothesis.

expected_control_group_cases.description: Expected number of cases in the control group under the null hypothesis.

expected_control_group_noncases.description: Expected number of noncases in the control group under the null hypothesis.

total_cases.description: Total number of cases across both groups.

total_noncases.description: Total number of noncases across both groups.

total_population.description: Total population size across both groups.

z_stat.description: Z-statistic value for the hypothesis test.

chi_square_statistic.description: Chi-square statistic calculated for the test.

degrees_of_freedom.description: Degrees of freedom for the chi-square test.
